title: unifend
text: unifend Shut down or suspend the Uniface environment started by the deprecated unifbeg function. Important: The unifend function is deprecated and should not be used. Instead, use the Call-In API, a collection of call-in functions related to the ProcScript activate command. For more information, see the "Call-In Using the Call-In API" topic. int unifend (level); Copy Parameters level Number specifying whether Uniface should be shut down or suspended. Table 1. Level for unifend Value of Level Meaning -1 Shut down Uniface and terminate the program. 0 Return control of keyboard to the 3GL application. 1 Clear screen and return control of keyboard and screen to the 3GL application. Return Values Table 2. Values returned by unifend Value Meaning 1 The Uniface environment was successfully shut down. Use Library: ulib Description unifend can be called from a 3GL call-in application to shut down or suspend the Uniface environment. If the level parameter is -1, unifend will shut down the Uniface environment as well as terminate the program (so control is not passed back to your application). If level is one, the screen is cleared and control of the keyboard and screen is returned to the calling 3GL. Additionally, for a character-mode application only, the *Busy* indicator will be turned off, and the cursor will be positioned on the bottom line of the screen. The Uniface environment must be reinitialized with unifbeg if Uniface service routines are required after a unifend . Example: unifbeg, unifend, and urun for simple call-in The following C code performs a straight-forward call-in: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { /* Tell unifbeg to use myapp.aps/myapp.asn */ usetparm(0, "myapp.aps", ""); unifbeg(0); /* Start Uniface interactive environment */ urun("FRM", 0, 0, 0, 0); /* Run form FRM with default size/position */ unifend(-1); /* Shut down the Uniface environment */ } Copy Related Links Call-In Using the Call-In API The Uniface 3GL Call-in API is a collection of call-in functions related to the ProcScript activate command.
